steps.title: Set off though
steps.content: The sentence uses though as a parenthetical adverb meaning however. It needs to be set off after pristine before the sentence moves to the next independent clause.

steps.title: Break before the next clause
steps.content: A puts the semicolon before though, C adds only commas before the next independent clause, and D omits the needed clause break. A comma plus semicolon sets off though and separates the clauses. The answer is B.
